description_long: This is a rustic log cabin with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in irregular, overlapping wooden shingles. The roof features a prominent, slightly bent chimney emitting a series of smoke puffs, suggesting an active fireplace inside. The cabin's walls are constructed from horizontally stacked logs, with visible end joints and rounded log edges. At one gable end, there is a single, rectangular wooden door with a simple handle, framed by the log structure. The opposite gable end is solid, with no visible openings or architectural features. The overall appearance is weathered and hand-built, evoking a traditional, rural dwelling suited for forested or mountainous environments.
description: A rustic log cabin with a steep gable roof of wooden shingles, a smoking chimney, and a single wooden door at one end. The structure is made of stacked logs and has a handcrafted, traditional look.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from the side, showcasing the textured wooden shingle roof, the bent chimney with smoke, and the log wall construction.
description_view_1: This view highlights the gable end with the single wooden door, framed by rounded logs and the protruding roof ridge.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the cabin, similar to view 0, displays the shingled roof and log wall, but without any visible openings.
description_view_3: The rear gable end is visible, showing a solid log wall with no doors or windows, and the continuation of the roof's ridge.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with irregular wooden shingles and a single chimney
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin
attribute_door_placement: A single rectangular door is centered on one gable end of the cabin
